prep.summary: Synthesize key topics from the last seven days to prepare for your staff meeting—focusing on critical architectural shifts and severe capacity constraints. You must address team burnout following the departures of Unsworth and Fred Diel—while aligning with Deepak to clarify decision-making authority in the P&C space. Track the active technical streams—specifically the "quick and dirty" chatbot prototypes using the existing billing codebase, the Skan.ai task mining pilot, and the security hurdles delaying the claims two-way texting rollout.
tasks: Align with Deepak to define clear decision-making accountability within the P&C architecture space to mitigate organizational complexity.
Finalize the technical approach for the upcoming chatbot prototype using the existing bill pay design rather than custom-building toolsets.
Resolve the security and compliance hurdles regarding PII/NPI sharing that are currently stalling the HiMarley visual voicemail integration.
Consolidate the cross-divisional business cases for Skan.ai task mining into a unified P&C view to support the planned autumn pilot.
Prepare the required architecture presentation materials for Suhas's upcoming planning meeting.
prep.title: Weekly Staff – P&C CTO Leadership

prep.summary: Reviewing your recent work notes reveals no record of your last meeting with Dan Weaver or any recent mentions of ServiceNow—you must prepare to address these topics without historical context. For P&C stability and obsolescence, your notes highlight a capacity crisis on the architecture team—hindering modernization—alongside critical caution phases in Claims Evolution due to security and compliance hurdles. Address these structural bottlenecks—specifically the lack of clear decision-making authority—to unblock vulnerability remediation and platform stability initiatives.
tasks: Ask Dan for his direct updates on ServiceNow roadmap alignment.
Align with Deepak to define clear decision-making authority within the P&C architecture space.
Address the severe capacity constraints on your architecture team—mitigating burnout to free up resources for obsolescence remediation.
Review the security and compliance hurdles stalling the Claims Evolution rollout.
